resps:
Let the initial number of trees Haley had be $I$.
Given that $I = 11$.
The number of trees that died after the typhoon is 2.
Let the number of trees that died be $D = 2$.
The number of trees that survived the typhoon is the initial number of trees minus the number of trees that died.
Number of trees survived $= I - D = 11 - 2 = 9$.
We want to find how many more trees survived the typhoon than those that died.
This is the difference between the number of trees that survived and the number of trees that died.
Difference $= \text{Number of trees survived} - \text{Number of trees died} = 9 - 2 = 7$.

The answer is 7.
